description: Advances: specled.package — advisory findings from the specled_-6fn test-critic pass, deferred out of that ticket as future hardening of test/docs_identifier_lint_test.exs. (A3) The token patterns lead with a (?<![\w/]) negative lookbehind to keep a finding-code-shaped token from matching inside a file path. Its bounds are unbounded and untested: ANY token preceded by / is skipped, so e.g. foo/append_only/deep_fabricated is not flagged. Add boundary tests pinning both directions — the closure-file path segment .../config/branch_guard_test.exs stays suppressed AND a real backtick-delimited code is still caught — so tightening or loosening the lookbehind cannot silently change coverage. (A4) severity_corpus/0 deliberately excludes .spec/** because spec scenarios quote atom-form config as the input under test; real atom-form severities exist at .spec/specs/prose_guard.spec.md, branch_guard.spec.md and spec_drift_trailer.spec.md, so the exclusion is load-bearing but unasserted. Assert the asymmetry: an atom-form severity in a guidance doc trips, the same form under .spec/** does not. Collapsing severity_corpus into finding_code_corpus would start false-positiving with no test warning. (A5) The token char-class [a-z_] cannot represent a finding code containing a digit. Not a current defect (no emitted code has one), but if one is ever added the hand-maintained @known_codes mirror and the extraction pattern silently diverge. Decide whether to widen the class or assert that no emitted code contains a digit.

comments.text: Additional findings from the specled_-6fn ship-it critical review (2026-07-23), same hardening theme: (1) COVERAGE GAP — the lint guards 3 of at least 5 emitted code namespaces; evidence/*, detector_unavailable and spec_requirement_too_short are unguarded (the corpus happens to be clean today). Either widen @token_patterns to cover them or narrow the must to claim only what is guarded. (2) STALE-MARKER CHECK — nothing detects an allow-marker whose token has since become a real emitted code, or whose token no longer appears on its line; such a marker silently rots into a permanent unearned exemption. (3) MIRROR IS FAIL-LOUD ONLY ONE WAY — @known_codes catches a code ADDED to lib/ but not one DELETED from it: docs naming a now-dead code keep passing. (4) PRE-EXISTING SPEC INACCURACY (not introduced by 6fn, deliberately left rather than silently widened) — .spec/specs/triangulation.spec.md scenario given:/when: claim a mix spec.check branch run with mix spec.cover.test fixture coverage that the covering test (test/integration/scenario_test_only_change_test.exs) never performs; it calls CoverageTriangulation.findings/3 on hand-built maps. 6fn reconciled only the then: clause, which was the fabricated-code defect in scope. Note append_only detect_scenario_regression/3 compares scenario COUNTS, so it cannot see then-clause content — a clean spec.check is weak evidence for scenario-text accuracy.

title: Harden doc-identifier lint: lookbehind bounds, severity-corpus asymmetry, digit-bearing codes
